message:
feat(fcp-auth-schema): new crate with AuthClaims + canonical CBOR (br-8n0rm.3)

New workspace member `fcp-auth-schema` ships:

- `labels::{cwt_claims, fcp2_claims}` — CBOR integer label constants
  (moved from fcp-crypto::cose, adds SCHEMA_VERSION=-65552 for 8n0rm.5)
- `AuthClaims` — typed claim-set struct with every field from the
  schema inventory (8n0rm.1), including delegation_depth + parent_token
  which previously required the .custom() escape hatch
- `AuthClaims::to_canonical_cbor` / `from_canonical_cbor` — sorted-by-
  label serde that is deterministic across runs. Entries use a
  BTreeMap<i64, Value> keyed by label, so field declaration order
  cannot affect the wire format.

Properties enforced by tests (12 green):
- empty() stamps CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION (=1)
- Round-trip lossless for empty / CWT-only / FCP2-full claim sets
- Serialization is deterministic (a=b=c across repeat calls)
- Wire-format labels emitted in ascending integer order
- Absent optionals + empty collections OMITTED from wire
- Unknown labels silently ignored on decode (forward-compat)
- Type mismatches surface helpful UnexpectedType errors
- FCP2 labels are unique + stay in negative half

grants + constraints are Vec<ciborium::Value> / Option<ciborium::Value>
opaque so this crate does NOT need to depend on fcp-core. fcp-core's
CapabilityVerifier will deserialize those with its own typed knowledge
in 8n0rm.4.

Deps: serde + ciborium + chrono + thiserror only. No fcp-* deps —
this is the bottom of the auth stack.

Unblocks 8n0rm.4 (fcp-crypto refactor) and 8n0rm.5 (schema_version
policy).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+[package]
+name = "fcp-auth-schema"
+version = "0.1.0"
+edition = "2024"
+license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
+description = "FCP typed capability-token claim schema shared between fcp-crypto (builder) and fcp-core (verifier)"
+
+[lints]
+workspace = true
+
+[dependencies]
+serde.workspace = true
+ciborium.workspace = true
+chrono.workspace = true
+thiserror.workspace = true
+
+[dev-dependencies]
+# (none yet; tests use only std)

@@ -0,0 +1,46 @@
+//! # fcp-auth-schema
+//!
+//! Typed capability-token claim schema shared between `fcp-crypto`
+//! (builder side) and `fcp-core` (verifier side). Owns the single
+//! source of truth for the auth-critical claim layout.
+//!
+//! ## Background
+//!
+//! Before this crate existed, `fcp-crypto::cose::CapabilityTokenBuilder`
+//! and `fcp-core::CapabilityVerifier` each knew about FCP claim labels
+//! by integer constant, and drift between them was caught only by
+//! runtime tests (or worse, by a verification failure in production).
+//!
+//! This crate owns:
+//!
+//! 1. The CBOR integer label constants (`cwt_claims`, `fcp2_claims`)
+//! 2. The typed `AuthClaims` struct
+//! 3. Canonical-CBOR serialization (deterministic, sorted by label
+//!    integer) and its inverse.
+//!
+//! `fcp-crypto` consumes `AuthClaims` to build COSE_Sign1 token bytes.
+//! `fcp-core` parses signed-bytes payload into `AuthClaims` to run
+//! capability verification against typed fields.
+//!
+//! ## Dependency direction
+//!
+//! ```text
+//! fcp-auth-schema ──┬── fcp-crypto
+//!                   │
+//!                   └── fcp-core (also depends on fcp-crypto)
+//! ```
+//!
+//! No cycles. `fcp-auth-schema` has no dependency on `fcp-crypto` or
+//! `fcp-core`; it is the bottom of the auth stack.
+//!
+//! See also:
+//! - `docs/architecture/adr/8n0rm-claim-schema-inventory.md`
+//! - `docs/architecture/adr/8n0rm-typed-claims.md`
+
+#![forbid(unsafe_code)]
+#![warn(missing_docs)]
+
+pub mod claims;
+pub mod labels;
+
+pub use claims::{AuthClaims, SchemaError};
